regulation_reference: SEBI ICDR Regulations 2018, Chapter IX, Schedule VI, Part A, Clause 17
description: Disclosure of all pending litigation, regulatory actions, and legal proceedings involving the company, its promoters, directors, and group companies that are material or could affect the company's operations.

case_type.label: Case Type
case_type.icdr_text: All pending litigations involving the issuer, promoters, directors, and group companies shall be disclosed and categorized.
case_type.help_text: Select the type of legal proceeding.

party_involved.label: Party Involved
party_involved.icdr_text: Details of parties involved including whether it is against the company, its promoter, director, or group company.
party_involved.help_text: Name the parties involved (e.g., 'Company vs. XYZ Authority' or 'ABC vs. Promoter').

court_or_authority.label: Court / Authority / Forum
court_or_authority.icdr_text: Name of the court, tribunal, authority, or forum where the proceeding is pending.
court_or_authority.help_text: Name of the court, tribunal, or regulatory authority.

status.label: Current Status
status.icdr_text: Current status of the proceeding and the next date of hearing, if applicable.
status.help_text: Current status of the case (e.g., pending hearing, order reserved, appeal filed).

amount_involved.label: Amount Involved (₹ in Lakhs)
amount_involved.icdr_text: The amount involved in such proceedings, if quantifiable.
amount_involved.help_text: Financial exposure or amount claimed, if quantifiable. Leave blank if not applicable.

notes: SEBI defines materiality based on the SEBI Board decision on 'Material Litigation Policy' disclosure requirements.
Criminal proceedings against promoters and directors must be disclosed regardless of materiality.
Tax proceedings are separately categorized as they represent contingent liabilities.
Disclose cases filed BY the company as well as AGAINST the company.
